name: Grand Marnier Flan Cake
recipeInstructions:
------------------------CAKE BATTER------------------------
3/4 cup Sifted cake flour
1/2 cup Sugar
1 teaspoon Baking powder
1/4 teaspoon Salt
2 Egg yolks
3 tablespoons Oil
1 tablespoon Grand Marnier
3 tablespoons Orange juice
3 Egg whites
1/4 teaspoon Cream of tartar
-------------------------RUM GLAZE-------------------------
1 pint Syrup
2 tablespoons Sugar
1 tablespoon Cornstarch
1 tablespoon Dark rum
1 tablespoon Butter
Caramelize 1/2 cup sugar in a heavy skillet or saucepan until deep amber. Quickly pour into an oval-shaped baking dish, covering the bottom evenly. In a bowl, beat together egg yolks, eggs, remaining 1/2 cup sugar, condensed milk, half and half, orange peel, and 1 tablespoon Grand Marnier. Pour this mixture over the caramel in the baking dish.
Prepare Cake Batter: Gently mix into the flan mixture with the beaten egg whites and cream of tartar until foamy. Gradually add the remaining 1/2 cup sugar, beating until stiff but not dry. Carefully fold the batter into the mixture.
Blend the reserved pineapple syrup, sugar, cornstarch, and rum in a small saucepan. Heat over low heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and clear. Stir in butter. Allow to cool for 5 minutes.
Pour the remaining liqueur over the cake. Chill, covered, until ready to serve. Invert onto a serving platter and garnish with berries, oranges, or other fresh fruit. Makes 10 to 12 servings.
